behaviors.description: The embedding vector produced for a given query string has the correct dimensionality for the vector database index.
behaviors.justification: Embedding model must be called with actual input to verify output dimensionality matches database expectations. A mock embedding service always passes regardless of real dimensionality. Calling the actual embedding service (local library or API) is the only way to catch dimension mismatch failures.
behaviors.test_description: Call the actual embedding model with representative query strings and assert returned vectors match the expected dimensionality (e.g., 768 or 1024) required by the vector database index.

behaviors.description: The similarity search returns results ordered by descending cosine similarity score.
behaviors.justification: Vector database must execute real search with real data to verify sort ordering. Mocking the database always passes; only calling an actual vector DB instance reveals ordering bugs. Must test against real database behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Insert test vectors with known similarity relationships into a real vector database instance, perform similarity search, and verify results are strictly ordered by descending cosine similarity scores.

behaviors.description: The LLM re-ranking step changes the order of at least some results compared to the raw similarity ranking when the query has nuanced intent.
behaviors.justification: Real LLM required; mocking the LLM hides the entire failure. LLM outputs are non-deterministic and depend on actual model behavior, not infrastructure. The failure 'LLM re-ranking doesn't work' can only be caught by calling the real model. System-level infrastructure alone doesn't detect model behavior failures.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real LLM re-ranker with nuanced queries (e.g., queries with contextual or implicit intent) and similarly-scored results; verify output ranking differs from input, accounting for non-determinism across multiple runs.

behaviors.description: A query with no close matches in the database returns an empty result set rather than low-relevance noise.
behaviors.justification: Real vector database with real search behavior required to verify threshold/filtering logic. Mocking the database won't reveal filtering bugs. Must test against actual database with realistic data distribution to catch cases where unrelated results are incorrectly returned.
behaviors.test_description: Insert test vectors into a real vector database, then search with a query vector orthogonal or far from all stored vectors, and assert the result set is empty or falls below the configured relevance threshold.

behaviors.description: The end-to-end latency from receiving the query to returning re-ranked results stays below 2 seconds for the median case.
behaviors.justification: Real external API calls (embedding service, vector database, LLM re-ranker) required to measure actual network and inference latency. Local mocks would execute quickly and mask bottlenecks. System-level real HTTP/API calls capture genuine latency profile. If LLM non-determinism significantly affects latency distribution, Agentic/Workflow provide deeper visibility.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the full end-to-end pipeline with real embedding service, vector database, and LLM re-ranker; measure wall-clock time from query receipt to final re-ranked results; run multiple iterations and verify the median latency (across runs) stays below 2 seconds.
